Facts.text: Constantinople, the capital of the Byzantine Empire, is significantly damaged by a 6.4 magnitude earthquake
Uthman ibn Affan, companion of Muhammad, is appointed the 3rd Caliph of Islam (644-56)
Chinese Emperor Wenzong conspires with chancellor Li Xun and general Zheng Zhu to kill all the eunuchs, but the plot is foiled. Also known as the Sweet Dew Incident
Adrian II begins his reign as Catholic Pope
John VIII elected as Catholic Pope
Theobald Buccapecus elected Pope Coelestinus II (he refuses)
During St. Lucia's Flood in Northwest Netherlands the Zuiderzee seawall collapses with loss of over 50,000 lives. Fifth largest recorded flood in history
Polish Parliament selects Istvan Bathory as king of Poland
Sea battle at Manila Bay between Olivier van Noort's Dutch ships and a Spanish fleet, Dutch sink the Spanish galleon San Diego, 350 die
Artificial pearls 1st manufactured by M. Jacquin in Paris made of gypsum pellets covered with fish scales
The Forty-seven Ronin (leaderless samurai), under the command of Ōishi Kuranosuke, avenge the death of their master in Japan
Prosper Jolyot de Crébillon's play "Electre" premieres in Paris
The first military academy in the world, the Theresian Military Academy, is founded in Austria
First incident of American Revolution - 400 New Hampshire militiamen successfully attack Fort William and Mary
Charleston, South Carolina evacuated by the British
1st state road authorized, Frankfort, Kentucky, to Cincinnati
David Wilkinson of Rhode Island patents a nut & bolt machine
The Battle of Lake Borgne in Louisiana: British victory over the United States
Alabama admitted to Union as 22nd state
The Toledo War unofficially ends, boundary dispute between Ohio state and territory of Michigan
First chamber music group in the US gives its first concert in Boston
Battle of Kinston, fought in Lenoir County, North Carolina begins (Goldsboro Expedition), Union victory (US Civil War)
Battle of Bean's Station-Confederacy repulses Union in Tennessee
American Academy of Political & Social Science organized in Philadelphia

Day 1 of first cricket test between Australia and England finishes with Australia 5-346, debut of future Australian captain Joe Darling, great all-rounder Ernie Jones and outstanding England batsman Archie MacLaren;
The Glasgow Underground Railway is opened by the Glasgow District Subway Company
Birth of Quantum Physics: German Physicist Max Planck presents a theoretical derivation of his black-body radiation law
France and Italy sign a secret agreement by which Italy recognizes France's right to exploit Morocco in return for France's conceding her the same right in Tripoli
1st table tennis tournament is held at the London Royal Aquarium
England cricket batsman R. E. Foster completes 287 on debut in 1st Test against Australia at the Sydney Cricket Ground
The Wright brothers attempt to fly the Wright Flyer for the first time at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina
The schooner Thomas W. Lawson runs aground and founders near the Hellweather's Reef within the Scilly Isles in a gale. The pilot and 15 seamen die.
Greece formally takes possession of Crete
Lisandro de la Torre and others found the Democratic Progressist Party (Partido Demócrata Progresista, PDP) at the Hotel Savoy, Buenos Aires.
America's first board certified doctors are named by the American Board of Ophthalmology following an examination at the University of Tennessee Medical School in Memphis, Tennessee
UFA, Universal Film AG, forms in Germany
David Loyd George's coalition Government wins a clear majority in the UK general election
Sinn Féin candidates win 73 of 105 Irish seats in UK general election, though all will refuse to take seats in Westminster, instead meeting at a Dail Eireann in Dublin
Respighi's symphony "Pini di Roma" premieres in Paris
Alban Berg's first opera "Wozzeck" premieres with the Berlin State Opera in Berlin, Germany
Alban Berg's first opera, "Wozzeck" premieres at the Berlin State Opera.
Thomas Madsen-Mygdal head of the Liberal Party forms a government in Denmark
Iraq gains independence from Britain, but British troops remain
Alexander Zaimis elected president of Greece
NY Giants defeat Notre Dame 22-0 in a charity game
1st assembly of Anton Musserts NSB in Utrecht
1st streamlined steam locomotive introduced in Albany, NY
Test Cricket debut of "Chuck" Fleetwood-Smith v South Africa, Durban
AL permits Cleveland & Philadelphia to play night games
Will Harridge is elected to a 10-year-term as AL president
The League of Nations expels the Soviet Union for invading Finland
1st NFL division playoff, Bears beat Packers 33-14
Japan and Thailand sign a treaty of alliance
U-557 torpedoes British cruiser Galatea
Beginning of Liese-Aktion: Germany tries to recruit Dutch men for the Germany labor force
German occupiers forbid use of electricity in parts of Holland
US Congress establishes rank of General of Army (5-star General)
General Assembly of United Nations votes to construct headquarters in New York City
Togo made a trusteeship territory of UN
The National Association for Stock Car Auto Racing (NASCAR) is founded in Daytona Beach, Florida
Baseball owners choose Lou Perini (Braves), Phil Wrigley (Cubs), Del Webb (Yankees), & Ellis Ryan (Indians) to select new commissioner
UN General Assembly establishes High Commission for Refugees, goes on to win Nobel Peace Prize in 1954
Deepak Shodhan scores 110 on Test Cricket debut for India v Pakistan, Calcutta
KROD (now KDBC) TV channel 4 in El Paso, TX (CBS) begins broadcasting
UN Troops kill 82 North Korean POWs during a prison camp riot in Pongam-do, South Korea
WOAY TV channel 4 in Oak Hill-Beckley, WV (ABC) begins broadcasting
16 countries join the United Nations, including Austria, Finland, Italy, and Spain
Tappan Zee Bridge in NY opens to traffic
Paul-Henri Spaak of Belgium is selected to become Secretary-General of NATO
Archbishop Makarios proclaimed president of Cyprus
Joe B. Jordan in F-104C starfighter sets world altitude record, 31,513m
The album "Time Out" by the Dave Brubeck Quartet is released, peaking at No. 2 on the Billboard pop albums chart, and becomes the first jazz album to sell a million copies.
Australia v West Indies 1st Test Cricket at the Gabba ends in a tie
Organization for Economic Cooperation & Development (OECD) forms
Washington Senators joins American League
Jimmy Dean's "Big Bad John" is 1st country song to get a gold record
Mariner 2 makes 1st US fly-by of another planet (Venus)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Verne Gagne beats The Crusher in Minneapolis, to become NWA champ
Michael Brown meets Renee Fladen, then writes "Walk Away Renee"
DNA created in a test tube
Bishen Bedi takes 7-98 (career-best) v Australia at Calcutta
Golden Gate Bridge lights out all night due to power failure
The Pakistan Army executes an estimated 1,111 of East Pakistan's intellectuals during the Bangladesh Liberation War
U.S. premiere of "Nicholas and Alexandra", film adaptation of Robert K. Massie's best selling novel
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Eugene Cernan & Harrison Schmitt leave the Moon
Islander Glenn Resch's 1st shut-out opponent-Kings 3-0
6 So Moluccan terrorists surrender, holding 23 hostages for 12 days
WCPR (Brooklyn New York pirate radio station) begins broadcasting on 1620 AM
Dutch 1st Chamber condemns Dutch Liberal/social dem abortion laws
Egyptian & Israeli representatives gather in Cairo for first formal peace conference
MLB Boston Red Sox trade Fergie Jenkins to Texas Rangers for John Poloni & cash
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Anders Kailur scores on 6th Islander penalty shot
Massacre at Bob's Big Boy restaurant in Los Angeles with 3 people killed and 6 injured
Minnesota Vikings pass for 456 yards against Cleveland Browns, winning 28-24
New Orleans Saints end 14 game losing streak, beat NY Jets 21-20
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Israel annexes Golan Heights (seized from Syria in war of 1967)
Sportscaster Howard Cosell retires from Monday Night Football
Experimental airplane Voyager, piloted by Dick Rutan & Jeana Yeager, takes off from Edwards AFB, California on 1st non-stop, non-refueled flight around the world; lands 9 days later
Chrysler pleads no contest to selling driven vehicles as new
CBS' $1.1 B bid wins exclusive 1990-94 major-league baseball rights
NBA's Miami Heat wins 1st game ever, 89-88 (Clippers), after 17 loses
Spanish General strike to protest austerity measures
US agrees to talk to Palestine Liberation Org for the 1st time in 13 yrs
Right to Die case permits Nancy Cruzan to have her feeding tube removed, she dies 12 days later
57th Heisman Trophy Award: Desmond Howard, Michigan (WR)
Ferry boat Salem Express sinks in the Red Sea, 476 killed
52 people, including 25 children, are killed when a helicopter from Tkvarcheli is shot down during the War in Abkhazia
Muslim fundamentalists murder 12 Croats and Bosnians in Algeria
Construction begins on the Three Gorges Dam, China
AIDS patient Jeff Getty receives baboon bone marrow transplant
62nd Heisman Trophy Award: Danny Wuerffel, Florida (QB)
64th Heisman Trophy Award: Ricky Williams, Texas (RB)
Flash floods in Venezuela after torrential rainfall results in the deaths of thousands of people and causes a total collapse in the state's infrastructure
President of Pakistan Pervez Musharaf narrowly escapes an assassination attempt
The Millau viaduct, the highest bridge in the world, designed by architect Norman Foster and engineer Michel Virlogeux, near Millau, France is officially opened
75th Heisman Trophy Award: Mark Ingram, Jr., Alabama (RB)
28 people, including 20 children, are shot to death at Sandy Hook Elementary School in Newtown, Connecticut
Australian Joel Parkinson wins the 2012 ASP World Tour
Gene Wolfe wins the Damon Knight Memorial Grand Master Award
Bus plunges off bridge into Balboa River near Argentina city of Rosario de la Frontera, killing 43 frontier police on board
Amazon announces its 1st delivery by drone 2 km from their warehouse in the UK
University of Toronto scientists present findings of the world's oldest water - 2 billion years old from a mine in Canada
Australian government establishes a Royal Commission into Misconduct in the Banking, Superannuation and Financial Services Industry
British surgeon Simon Bramhall admits to branding the liver of 2 patients with his initials
Suicide bomber kills at least 18 police officers at a training centre parade in Mogadishu, Somalia, Islamic group al-Shabab claim responsibility
The Walt Disney Company buys most of 21st Century Fox for $52.4 billion
Train ploughs into a school bus cutting it in half, killing 4 children and injuring 20 near Perpignan, France
US media regulator the Federal Communications Commission votes to end net neutrality
Report by Reuters US pharmaceutical company Johnson & Johnson knew for decades their talc was contaminated with asbestos
Bel Air mansion featured in TV series "The Beverly Hillbillies" sells for $150 million, becoming California's most expensive property
Miss World won by Miss Jamaica Toni-Ann Singh in London. First time all five major beauty titles held by black women.
America begins its first COVID-19 vaccinations using the Pfizer/BioNTech vaccine on the same day it records over 300,000 deaths
Russia revealed to have been behind massive cyberattack on US government agencies and private companies since the Spring
Total solar eclipse visible across southern Chile and Argentina
US Attorney General William Barr resigns
American Covid-19 death toll passes 800,000, with more deaths recorded in 2021 than 2020
Fuel tanker explosion after an accident leaves at least 90 dead in city of Cap-Haitien, Haiti
Steph Curry makes his 2,974th three-point shot to pass Ray Allen as the NBA's greatest career 3-point shooter during Golden State Warriors' 105-96 win over NY Knicks at Madison Square Garden
HSBC, Europe's largest bank, says it will stop financing new oil and gas fields, in attempt to lower global greenhouse gas emissions
Peru declares 30-day nationwide state of emergency, following a week of political protests after the removal and arrest of former President Pedro Castillo
PresidentJoe Bidenvows to extend US involvement in Africa in address at US-Africa Leaders summit in Washington, amid competition from China on the continent
European Union agrees to open membership negotiations with Ukraine at a summit on Brussels
90th Heisman Trophy Award: Travis Hunter, University of Colorado (WR/CB)
Cyclone Chido makes a direct hit on the French island territory of Mayotte off the coast of east Africa before striking northern Mozambique
PrincessMary Stuartsucceeds her fatherJames Vand becomes Queen Mary I of Scotland at 6 days old
The French invasion of Russia, led byNapoleon Bonaparte, officially ends with the French having lost as many as 530,000 people
War of the Triple Alliance: Paraguay invades the Brazilian province of Mato Grosso
Henry Morton Stanleyreturns to Brussels from the Congo
Emilio Aguinaldosigns the Pact of Biak-na-Bato, to end hostilities and dissolve his government, in exchange for amnesty and "$800,000 (Mexican)" as an indemnity
The Pure Food and Drug Act is introduced in the U.S. Senate, after lobbying by businessmanHenry John Heinz
US PresidentWoodrow Wilsonsigns Harrison Narcotics Tax Act, to regulate and tax production, importation, and distribution of opiates and coca products
Giacomo Puccini's opera "Il Trittico" premieres in NYC
Jack DempseyKOs Bill Brennan in 12 for heavyweight boxing title in NYC
French government ofÉdouard Herriotfalls
Josephine Bakerperforms in Amsterdam
Major League Baseball agrees on use of a standard ball; disagrees on increasing rosters from 23 to 25 players, although CommissionerKenesaw Mountain Landiseventually decides on 25
PremierWinston Churchilltravels to US on board HMS Duke of York
Elmer Rice's play "Dream Girl" premieres in NYC
Brooklyn Dodgers sign pitcherSandy Koufax
Pat Summerallkicks game winning field goal for the New York Giants against the Cleveland Browns at Yankee Stadium
Canadian Prime MinisterLester B. Pearsonannounces that he is retiring from politics, succeeded by Pierre Trudeau
Bobby Orrscores first career hat trick (vs Chicago Blackhawks)
"Leaving on a Jet Plane" single sung byPeter, Paul and Mary, written byJohn Denver, hits #1 on US Billboard's Hot Top 100
Jackson Five make their 1st appearance on "TheEd SullivanShow"
Disaster film "The Towering Inferno" is released, starringSteve McQueenandPaul Newman
Dutch war criminalPieter Mentensentenced in Amsterdam to 15 years
Test Cricket debut of Pakistan spin bowling greatAbdul Qadirv England in 1st Test at Lahore
At 2 PM EST there is 10 minutes of silence in memory ofJohn Lennon
LA Kings'Marcel Dionnebecomes 9th NHLer to score 500 goals
Biographical drama film "Silkwood", directed byMike Nichols, and starringMeryl Streep,Kurt Russell, andCherpremieres
US Secretary of StateGeorge P. Shultzarrives in West Berlin
Allan Borderscores 205 vs NZ to become Australian crickets' top run getter
Louis Jordan's revue "Five Guys Named Moe" premieres in London
Lennox Lewisgiven WBC title, whenRiddick Bowerefused to fight him
"Philadelphia", one of the first mainstream films about AIDS, directed by Jonathan Demme, starringTom HanksandDenzel Washington, premieres in Century City, California
Phoenix Coyote wingerMike Gartnerbecomes 5th NHLer to score 700 goals
US PresidentGeorge W. Bushannounces the capture ofSaddam Hussein

PresidentGeorge W. Bushmake his fourth and final trip to Iraq as president and is almost struck by two shoes thrown at him by Iraqi journalist Muntadhar al-Zaidi during a farewell conference in Baghdad
Venkatraman Ramakrishnanis named as one of the 25 Greatest Global Living Indians by NDTV Channel, India
Shinzō Abeand his ruling Liberal Democratic Party win re-election in Japan, retaining their two-thirds majority with coalition partner New Kōmeitō Party
"Star Wars: The Force Awakens" directed byJ.J. Abramsand starringDaisy Ridley,John BoyegaandHarrison Fordhas its world premiere at the TCL Chinese Theatre in Los Angeles
Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Bruce Springsteen,James Taylor, Ronnie Spector, Idina Menzal, Darlene Love, and Jennifer Nettles
The Vatican announces it has rediscovered the lost last paintings ofRaphaelin Vatican Museum, painted in 1520
85th Heisman Trophy Award:Joe Burrow, Louisiana State University (QB); highest percentage of first place votes ever with 90.7%
US Electoral College votes 306-232 to officially affirmJoe Biden's presidential election victory
Lewis Koumasis an unused substitute in a Europa League match against Union SG
South Korean PresidentYoon Suk Yeolis impeached by a vote in the National Assembly after his failed attempt to impose martial law
Zealand, Brabant Netherlands adopt Gregorian calendar, tomorrow is 12/25
NorwegianRoald Amundsen's Antarctic expedition is the first to reach the South Pole
The Dayton Agreement is signed in Paris by leaders of various governments ending the conflict in the former Yugoslavia includingSlobodan Milošević, Alija Izetbegović, Franjo Tuđman and Bill Clinton
Go-Suzaku, 69th Emperor of Japan (1036-45), born in Heian Kyō, Japan (d. 1045)
